feature_gaps.description: Very few tools (only AgentOps, Maxim AI, Evidently, Virtue AI, Datadog) support multi-agent workflows. No tool comprehensively tests inter-agent coordination, shared state conflicts, message passing correctness, and emergent behaviors in multi-agent systems.
feature_gaps.current_coverage: 5 of 19 tools offer basic multi-agent support, but none deeply address orchestration testing

feature_gaps.gap: Session replay and time-travel debugging for agents
feature_gaps.description: Only AgentOps offers session replay. Most tools provide traces but cannot replay an agent session step-by-step to reproduce and debug issues interactively.

feature_gaps.gap: User persona simulation and behavioral testing
feature_gaps.description: Only Maxim AI offers user persona simulation. Teams cannot easily test how agents behave with adversarial users, confused users, domain experts, non-native speakers, etc.
Only 3 tools (Patronus AI, Portkey, Galileo) offer real-time guardrails that can block problematic outputs before reaching users. Most tools only evaluate after the fact.
Only 3 tools (Patronus AI, Portkey, Datadog) provide compliance/audit features. As AI regulation increases globally, there is a major gap in audit-ready evaluation tooling for regulated industries.
Only 4 tools (LangSmith, Langfuse, Patronus AI, Galileo, Maxim AI) support structured human annotation. Most tools are fully automated with no human-in-the-loop evaluation capability.

feature_gaps.gap: Automated regression detection across model updates
feature_gaps.description: While some tools offer basic comparison, no tool provides robust automated regression detection that alerts when model updates, prompt changes, or provider version bumps cause quality degradation in production.
feature_gaps.current_coverage: Partial support in ~8 tools, but none deeply solve this

segment_gaps.gap: No-code/low-code evaluation platform for non-technical users
segment_gaps.description: Almost all tools are developer-focused (CLI, SDK, Python). Product managers, QA analysts, domain experts, and business stakeholders cannot participate in evaluation without engineering support. Only Maxim AI claims low-code support.
Many tools are either open-source (requiring DevOps investment) or enterprise-priced. Mid-market companies lack affordable, managed evaluation platforms with reasonable pricing.
segment_gaps.current_coverage: Few tools in the $20-100/mo range with full feature sets

segment_gaps.description: Healthcare, finance, legal, and government sectors need domain-specific evaluation metrics, compliance checks, and audit trails. Current tools are domain-agnostic.
segment_gaps.current_coverage: No tools specialize in regulated-industry AI evaluation

segment_gaps.gap: Evaluation tooling for voice/multimodal AI agents
segment_gaps.description: All current tools focus on text-based LLM evaluation. Voice agents, vision-language agents, and multimodal systems lack specialized testing tools.
segment_gaps.current_coverage: 0 of 19 tools explicitly support multimodal agent evaluation

workflow_gaps.gap: End-to-end agent lifecycle testing (dev to prod to monitoring)
workflow_gaps.description: Most tools specialize in either development-time evaluation OR production monitoring. No single tool seamlessly covers the full lifecycle: develop -> test -> deploy -> monitor -> detect regression -> fix -> re-test.
workflow_gaps.current_coverage: Langfuse and LangSmith come closest but still have gaps

workflow_gaps.gap: Automated test generation from production failures
workflow_gaps.description: When agents fail in production, there is no tool that automatically converts production failures into regression test cases. Giskard does this for detected issues, but not from live production incidents.
workflow_gaps.current_coverage: Giskard offers partial support; no tool does this from production data

workflow_gaps.gap: Collaborative evaluation workflows (eng + PM + domain expert)
workflow_gaps.description: No tool provides good cross-functional collaboration where engineers define metrics, PMs set priorities, and domain experts annotate results in a unified workflow.
workflow_gaps.current_coverage: Orq.ai and Humanloop have basic collaboration; no tool truly solves this

integration_gaps.description: Most eval tools are tightly coupled to specific frameworks (LangSmith -> LangChain, Opik -> Comet, etc.). Teams using CrewAI, AutoGen, custom frameworks, or switching between frameworks lack a universal evaluation layer.
integration_gaps.current_coverage: Promptfoo, DeepEval, and Evidently are relatively framework-agnostic

integration_gaps.description: While several tools support CI/CD integration, none provide a deeply integrated experience that treats agent evaluation as a first-class CI/CD step with gate conditions, parallel eval, and automatic rollback.
integration_gaps.current_coverage: Promptfoo and DeepEval have basic CI/CD; others require manual setup

integration_gaps.gap: Unified observability across the full AI stack
integration_gaps.description: Teams use separate tools for LLM tracing, vector DB monitoring, tool-call validation, and application APM. No tool provides unified observability across the entire agent tech stack.
integration_gaps.current_coverage: Datadog comes closest with existing APM + LLM observability

pricing_gaps.description: Most tools charge per seat or per trace/event. For teams that run evaluations sporadically (e.g., before releases), a pay-per-evaluation model would be more cost-effective.
pricing_gaps.current_coverage: No tool offers pure pay-per-eval pricing

pricing_gaps.description: Many enterprise tools require 'contact sales' and annual contracts (Arize, Patronus, etc.). Enterprises wanting predictable budgets and self-serve procurement face friction.
pricing_gaps.current_coverage: Braintrust, Maxim AI have transparent pricing; most enterprise tools do not
